findings.summary: MCP ecosystem mapped: no IPC layer exists, SDK client communicates via HTTP REST API, dialog-select-mcp.tsx has WIP but no persistence/edit/delete/local-server support. Key decision: Option B (electron-store persistence)
Phase 1 custom agents pattern (electron-store + IPC handlers + preload bridge) is the established persistence pattern for desktop-managed configs
Edit dialog pattern established: Props with optional entry, onSave callback, dialog.close() — follow exactly for dialog-edit-mcp.tsx
MCP.Service.add() stores config in-memory only (s.config[name]) not persisted to config file. Option B electron-store bypasses this by providing desktop-only persistence layer

Plan revision 2 addresses all 3 blocking critic findings: ipc.ts state corrected, SDK API surface verified, destructive edits acknowledged. Also fixes window.api not electronAPI, inline confirmation pattern, structured error handling, createStore convention, language.t() usage, and discriminated union types.

SDK MCP API surface fully verified: Mcp class with status/add/connect/disconnect, Auth2 sub-client with remove/start/callback/authenticate. All methods exist with expected signatures. Config types McpLocalConfig and McpRemoteConfig verified as discriminated union.

Key convention misalignments fixed: window.api not window.electronAPI, inline confirmation pattern from dialog-manage-agents.tsx (no ConfirmDialog import), createStore not createSignal, language.t() not raw strings.

app.tsx Window interface already has getMcpServers/setMcpServers (dirty, added by another session). ipc.ts has RESERVED_STORE_NAMES and validation helpers at module level. dialog-select-mcp.tsx is 81% dirty with 261 uncommitted lines.
